foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Segmental anatomy determines abscess localization and guides percutaneous vs surgical drainage approach

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Portal pyaemia from intra-abdominal sepsis (e.g., appendicitis, diverticulitis) is a key pathogenic route for pyogenic liver abscess

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Hydatid cyst is the chief differential for cystic hepatic lesions; infected hydatid mimics pyogenic abscess

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Ascending cholangitis with biliary obstruction is the most common cause of pyogenic liver abscess

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Necrotic HCC can mimic liver abscess on imaging; tumor markers and biopsy differentiate the two

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Biliary obstruction predisposes to cholangitic abscesses; jaundice with fever and RUQ pain raises suspicion

objectives: SU28.10: Describe the applied anatomy of liver. Describe the Clinical features,
IM5.13: Enumerate the indications for ultrasound and other imaging studies
integration: This undergraduate-level roadmap connects Liver Abscess across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
